opp.name: Link Logistics- 2026 P2P
opp.ais_why:
• Only one Avoma touchpoint exists — a 15-minute silent ProcureCon meeting on 9/16/25 with Michael Wang — so no customer AI quote was captured.
• SF Description frames it as a possible P2P opp to augment an 'eclectic solution set' — incumbent tech is Blackrock and Coupa, with no AI language.
• Next steps in Feb and Mar focused on inviting Michael Wang to the Pinnacle panel and an NYC on-site to work through Blackrock/Coupa obstacles — AI has not surfaced as a decision criterion.

items.source: Zero recorded buyer calls or discovery conversations in 2026; only touchpoint is 16 Sep 2025 booth encounter (not recorded)
No pain, metrics, compelling event, or ROI documented in Salesforce or calls
No EB identified; Michael Wang is Decision Maker but budget sign-off chain unknown
No documented fit criteria, RFP scorecard, or Link priorities
No mapped process, committee, or timeline from Qualified to close

metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem or ROI case on record. The only buyer touchpoint was a brief conference booth encounter on 16 Sep 2025 (9+ months ago, not recorded), so no pain, savings target, cost reduction, cycle-time improvement, or headcount impact was captured. Salesforce carries no value articulation in any business-requirement or compelling-event field. With zero discovery calls or documented qualification conversations in 2026, the business case remains completely unbuilt. The immediate move is to re-engage Michael Wang (VP Procurement) with a discovery call that surfaces and quantifies the procurement pain Link is solving for, anchors it to a business outcome, and builds the financial justification required for a $250k investment with Merlin/AI in scope.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Michael Wang, VP Procurement, Decision Maker
Avoma: booth encounter 16 Sep 2025, not recorded, no follow-up logged
champion.narrative: No champion identified or developed. Michael Wang (VP Procurement, Decision Maker contact role) is the sole named buyer-side contact, but with zero engagement since the 16 Sep 2025 booth encounter (9+ months ago, not recorded) and no subsequent calls, emails, or documented advocacy, he has shown no active sponsorship or internal selling on Zycus's behalf. There is no evidence he has introduced other stakeholders, defended Zycus against Coupa or the 'Others' competitor, or driven the evaluation forward internally. The contact role label 'Decision Maker' suggests authority, but authority without advocacy is not a champion. The deal is single-threaded and dormant. The immediate move is to re-engage Michael with a high-value offer (Horizon invite per the 12 Jun Next Step is one vector; a discovery call offering procurement AI insights or a Merlin demo tailored to logistics/real-estate procurement is another) and assess his willingness to sponsor: will he bring in his team (the Procurement Analyst, the Procurement Managers on the account contact list), will he share internal priorities or competitive shortlist intel, will he advocate for Zycus in internal discussions. If he remains passive or unresponsive after re-engagement, the deal team must multi-thread immediately to the COO, CFO, or a peer VP who owns the pain more acutely and can champion from a different angle.

competition.narrative: Salesforce Competitors picklist lists 'Coupa' and 'Others', but no call, next-step, or task detail explains threat level, buyer sentiment, shortlist status, or our wedge. The booth encounter on 16 Sep 2025 (not recorded) offered no competitive intel. With zero discovery calls in 2026 and no documented RFP or vendor comparison, it is unknown whether Link is benchmarking live quotes from Coupa, whether 'Others' refers to the relevant stakeholder, Jaggaer, another peer, or a do-nothing/incumbent scenario, and whether Zycus is even shortlisted. The $250k scope with Merlin/AI suggests a differentiated play, but the positioning has not been tested or confirmed with the buyer. The immediate move is to surface the competitive landscape in the Michael Wang re-engagement call: ask directly who else Link is evaluating, how far those conversations have progressed, what Link likes or dislikes about each option (price, fit, AI, implementation), and where Zycus stands. Then position Merlin Intake and the agentic procurement AI narrative as the wedge against Coupa's legacy UI and manual workflows, and document the competitor status, sentiment, and threat level in Salesforce with verbatim buyer quotes.

identify_pain.narrative: No operational or financial pain articulated. The booth encounter on 16 Sep 2025 (not recorded) captured interest but no pain statement, and no subsequent discovery call or qualification conversation has occurred in the nine months since. Salesforce pain, compelling-event, and business-problem fields are empty. The product scope (eProcurement, eInvoicing, iSaaS, Merlin Intake) implies Link may be addressing procurement inefficiency, invoice cycle time, or supplier spend visibility, but the specific pain owner, the severity, the cost of inaction, and the urgency forcing a 2027 decision are all unknown. Without a named pain and a pain owner, the deal has no anchor and no champion-building path. The first action in any re-engagement must be a discovery call with Michael Wang (and ideally others on his procurement or finance team) to ask: what is broken today, what is it costing you (time, dollars, errors, compliance risk), who feels that pain most acutely, and what happens if you do nothing. Document the answers verbatim, quantify where possible, and tie the pain to the Merlin/AI value prop (e.g. Merlin Intake automating requisition routing and approvals, cutting cycle time and freeing procurement analyst capacity).

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics unknown. No MSA, SOW, Order Form, legal review, or InfoSec questionnaire workflow has been discussed or documented. The account roster includes a CISO (Jerread Wright) and a Legal Senior Manager (Josette Williams), signaling that security and legal approval will be required, but neither is tied to this opportunity and no paper-process conversation has occurred. With Merlin/AI in scope, an InfoSec deep-dive and data-handling review are likely mandatory, yet no timeline or owner for those gates is recorded. Since the last touchpoint was 9+ months ago and the deal has not advanced past Qualified, the paper process has not been initiated. The move is to surface this topic in the re-engagement call with Michael Wang: ask what approvals (legal, InfoSec, procurement policy, ERP integration) are standard for a SaaS+AI buy at Link, who owns each, and what lead time they require, then build those gates into the mutual close plan before entering any formal evaluation or proposal stage.

economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer identified. Michael Wang holds the Decision Maker contact role (VP Procurement), which suggests he controls the category and likely the commercial conversation, but no call or field evidence confirms budget authority, CFO/C-level sponsorship, or sign-off chain. Salesforce fields for sponsor, budget approval, and executive sponsor are empty. The account roster lists a COO (Kerry Hewson) and a VP Real Estate Operations (Amanda Boyd), either of whom may hold or influence the budget, but neither is mapped to this opportunity and neither has appeared on a call. With a $250k SaaS+Merlin deal and a 2027 close, the EB must be identified and engaged by mid-stage Qualified. The next move is to ask Michael directly who approves the spend and to request an introduction to that executive, then map the approval chain and validate active sponsorship before advancing to shortlist.

decision_process.narrative: No decision process mapped. The deal sits in Qualified with one Decision Maker contact role (Michael Wang, VP Procurement) but no visibility into the approval chain, committee structure, vendor selection timeline, or gates between now and close. Salesforce process and mandate fields are empty. The account contact list hints at a multi-stakeholder org (COO, CISO, CTO, CFP&A Director, Legal Senior Manager), but none are confirmed as participants in this P2P evaluation and no org chart or RACI has been documented. With 273 days to close and no logged buyer activity in 2026, it is unclear whether Link is running a formal RFP, a sole-source negotiation, or has paused the initiative. The next step is to ask Michael Wang directly: what does your internal process look like from here to signature, who else must approve or influence, and what is driving the March 2027 timeline. Map that sequence, identify the gates, and align our milestones to their process before investing further in demos or proposals.

decision_criteria.narrative: No evaluation criteria documented. The booth encounter on 16 Sep 2025 (not recorded) offered no insight into Link's RFP rubric, test-case priorities, or technical fit requirements. Salesforce business-requirement and top-challenges fields are empty. Product scope is defined (eProcurement, eInvoicing, iSaaS, Merlin, Merlin Intake) but the buyer's decision framework, weighting (price vs feature vs AI vs implementation speed vs partner support), and pass/fail gates are completely unknown. Without criteria the deal cannot be positioned or defended against Coupa or the unnamed 'Others' competitor. The immediate action is a discovery or scoping call with Michael Wang and any procurement/IT stakeholders to surface the formal evaluation scorecard, the must-haves vs nice-to-haves, and how AI/Merlin capabilities rank in their prioritization, then document those in Salesforce and shape the demo/proposal to that rubric.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.

ai_fit_signal.summary: No AI fit signal. AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, and AIS_Why__c are null in Salesforce. No discovery call or buyer conversation has surfaced Link's AI appetite, digital maturity, or interest in autonomous procurement agents. Cannot tier the buyer as AI Hungry, AI Curious, or AI Resistant without a qualification conversation.

deal_movement.summary: Deal created 17 Feb 2026, moved to Qualified same day or shortly after. Only recorded buyer touchpoint: 16 Sep 2025 conference booth encounter with Michael Wang (9+ months ago, not recorded, likely predates opp creation and may have been the trigger). No logged buyer activity, calls, emails, or stage progression in 2026. Last SF modification 11 Jun 2026. Next Step dated 12 Jun 2026 ('RH Invite Michael to Horizon') is rep-initiated outreach, not a buyer reply. Deal is dormant and cold for 9+ months with zero forward movement.

items.name: Michael Wang
items.risk: Single point of contact; silent for 9+ months; no advocacy or multi-thread cooperation demonstrated; if unresponsive to re-engagement, deal is dead
items.title: Vice President, Procurement

items.detail: Single-threaded to one VP Procurement (Michael Wang, Decision Maker) with no Economic Buyer, no champion, no multi-thread, and 9+ months of silence. If Michael has deprioritized the initiative, left the company, or is no longer sponsor, the deal is lost and we have no alternate entry point. Account roster lists 20 Link contacts including C-level (COO, CISO, CTO) and procurement team, but none are engaged.

Close date 26 Mar 2027 (273 days out) with deal sitting in Qualified stage and zero forward progress in 2026. No discovery, demo, RFP, shortlist, or evaluation activity logged. Even with 9 months remaining, a cold deal in early Qualified with no buyer engagement, no MEDDPICC foundation, and no decision process has near-zero probability of closing on time—likely slips indefinitely or disqualifies.

No Economic Buyer identified, no budget sign-off chain mapped, no executive sponsor confirmed. $250k SaaS+Merlin deal requires C-level or senior VP approval, but the authority and funding source are completely unknown. Michael Wang (VP Procurement) may control category spend but likely does not hold final budget authority for a six-figure multi-year software investment.

No commercials, pricing, or ROI discussion on record. Amount $250k is likely a placeholder (round number, no line-item detail). With zero discovery calls and no quantified pain or business case, the buyer has no anchor for pricing expectations and no financial justification to defend the spend internally. Coupa is a named competitor; without a transparent pricing conversation and a differentiated ROI (Merlin AI value), Zycus risks being priced out or undercut.

Do-nothing / abandoned initiative risk. With 9 months of silence, no logged buyer activity, and no evidence the P2P project is live or funded, the highest probability outcome is that Link paused or cancelled the initiative and never informed Zycus. The deal may be a ghost opportunity carried in Pipeline because no disqualification conversation occurred.

champion_strength.source: SF OpportunityContactRole: Michael Wang, Decision Maker; Avoma: booth encounter 16 Sep 2025, zero engagement since
champion_strength.summary: No champion. Michael Wang (VP Procurement, Decision Maker contact role) is the only named buyer contact and has been silent for 9+ months since a brief conference booth encounter on 16 Sep 2025 (not recorded). No evidence of advocacy, internal selling, or willingness to multi-thread. Deal is single-threaded and dormant with no active sponsor.

items.action: Run direct re-qualification call with Michael Wang to confirm initiative is live and assess sponsor commitment.
items.trigger: 9-month silence since 16 Sep 2025 booth encounter; zero buyer engagement in 2026; Next Step 12 Jun 2026 Horizon invite yielded no response (14 days elapsed)
items.expected_effect: Determines whether Link's P2P initiative is live, paused, or cancelled. If live and Michael is responsive, moves deal to discovery/demo stage within 2 weeks. If unresponsive or initiative dead, disqualifies immediately and reallocates RH's capacity to active deals, preventing further wasted effort on a ghost opportunity.

items.action: Demand multi-thread introductions from Michael Wang to his procurement team and the Economic Buyer.
items.trigger: Single buyer contact role (Michael Wang, VP Procurement) on $250k Merlin deal; account roster lists 20 Link contacts including COO, CISO, CTO, procurement managers/analysts, none engaged; 9-month single-thread silence creates ghost/abandonment risk
items.expected_effect: Breaks single-thread dependency; builds parallel relationships with procurement team (Ian Mcintosh, Patrick Cullen, Riya Avaiya) who execute day-to-day and can champion internally if Michael deprioritizes. Surfaces the Economic Buyer (likely COO Kerry Hewson or C-level) who holds budget authority and can green-light the $250k investment. Reduces political risk and creates multiple advocates. If Michael refuses or delays introductions, signals weak sponsorship and supports a disqualify decision.

items.action: Deliver tailored Merlin Intake demo focused on logistics indirect procurement automation and quantified ROI.
items.trigger: Merlin + Merlin Intake in scope (SF Products) but zero positioning activity or buyer AI-appetite conversation; Coupa named competitor weak on autonomous AI; Link's logistics/real-estate business model implies high indirect procurement volume (facilities, MRO, services categories) suitable for Intake agent automation
items.expected_effect: Tests Link's AI appetite and use-case fit; positions Merlin Intake as the wedge against Coupa's static workflows by demonstrating autonomous requisition routing, approval acceleration, and procurement analyst time savings on Link's indirect categories. Quantifies ROI (cycle time reduction, FTE leverage, spend visibility) to build the business case. If Link shows strong interest, advances deal to the relevant stakeholder and anchors Merlin as must-have vs nice-to-have. If no interest, descopes Merlin and reprices, preventing a late-stage AI objection or price shock.

items.action: Surface and document the competitive shortlist, buyer sentiment, and Link's decision criteria in discovery call.
items.trigger: Coupa and 'Others' named in SF Competitors picklist but no buyer conversation, shortlist status, or threat-level intel on record; zero discovery calls means evaluation criteria, pricing benchmarks, and Link's must-haves are unknown
items.expected_effect: Reveals who Zycus is actually up against (confirms Coupa status, identifies 'Others' as Ariba/Jaggaer/GEP/etc or do-nothing), maps Link's evaluation rubric (price vs feature vs AI vs implementation speed vs partner), and surfaces any incumbent being replaced. Enables surgical competitive positioning (Merlin AI wedge vs Coupa, iSaaS speed vs Ariba complexity) and honest forecast adjustment (if Zycus is not shortlisted or criteria misalign, supports disqualify decision rather than chasing a lost deal for 9 more months).

items.action: Map the Economic Buyer, approval chain, and budget sign-off process with Michael Wang or a finance stakeholder.
items.trigger: $250k SaaS+Merlin investment requires C-level or senior VP budget approval but no EB identified, no sponsor confirmed, no sign-off chain documented; Michael Wang (VP Procurement, Decision Maker) likely does not hold final authority for six-figure software spend
items.expected_effect: Identifies the true Economic Buyer (likely COO Kerry Hewson, or a CFO/C-level), maps the approval gates (finance, legal, InfoSec, procurement policy), and confirms budget is allocated and the EB is an active sponsor. Prevents late-stage budget surprise or ghost EB blocking signature. If no EB emerges or budget is unfunded, supports early disqualify before investing in demos, RFP responses, or proposals.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 17 Feb 2026 (129 days ago), currently Qualified, close date 26 Mar 2027 (273 days out). Time in stage: 129 days with no progression. Last buyer touchpoint: 16 Sep 2025 conference booth encounter (9+ months ago, not_recorded). Last SF modification: 11 Jun 2026. Next Step dated 12 Jun 2026 is rep-initiated outreach (invite to Horizon), not a buyer reply. No LastActivityDate on record. No forward movement in 2026; deal sits in Qualified with no discovery, demo, or evaluation activity logged. A 9-month silence forces Off Track regardless of stage.

north_star_verdict.headline: Nine months of complete buyer silence since September 2025 conference encounter. No discovery, no calls, no engagement in all of 2026. Single-threaded to unresponsive procurement contact. Deal is functionally abandoned with zero forward momentum.

best_practice_check.summary: Deal is cold and single-threaded with no MEDDPICC foundation. Immediate re-qualification required.

competitive_position.summary: Coupa named in Salesforce as primary competitor; 'Others' also listed but unspecified. No buyer conversation, shortlist intel, or competitive sentiment on record. With zero discovery calls and a 9-month cold period, competitive standing is completely unknown. Coupa is a credible peer threat in mid-market P2P (strong brand, broad install base, mature eProcurement/eInvoicing), but Zycus's wedge—Merlin AI (Intake agent, negotiation agent, autonomous sourcing) and iSaaS implementation speed—has not been positioned or tested with Link. Until the deal is re-engaged and the buyer confirms shortlist status and evaluation criteria, Coupa must be treated as a high potential threat with no defense built. The single strongest current risk is 'do nothing' or ghost/abandoned initiative, not an active Coupa bake-off.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.how_we_win: Merlin AI (Intake automation, negotiation agent, agentic procurement vs Coupa's static workflows) plus iSaaS speed-to-value vs Coupa's long implementation cycles; position Merlin as the AI-first platform for logistics/real-estate indirect spend categories where Link needs procurement analyst leverage and cycle-time reduction. Must surface Link's decision criteria and test this wedge on a discovery call immediately.

competitors.name: Others (unspecified)
competitors.how_we_win: Unknown until 'Others' is identified; likely SAP Ariba, Jaggaer, Ivalua, or GEP. Surface the full shortlist in re-engagement call, then tailor Merlin positioning and iSaaS fit narrative to the named peer's weaknesses.

competitors.name: Do Nothing / Abandoned Initiative
competitors.source: Deal pulse: 9-month silence, no buyer engagement since Sep 2025
competitors.how_we_win: Re-anchor the business case with Michael Wang: surface the procurement pain, quantify the cost of inaction (cycle time, manual workload, compliance risk, lost savings), tie it to a compelling event or executive mandate, and present Merlin Intake as the lever that delivers fast ROI and frees his team's capacity. If no compelling event exists or Michael remains unresponsive, disqualify and reallocate resources.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Merlin and Merlin Intake are in scope (SF Products) but no positioning activity, discovery, demo, or buyer AI-appetite conversation has occurred. No AIS score on record. With zero calls and a 9-month cold period, Link's AI readiness, use-case fit, and willingness to pay for agentic procurement automation are completely unknown. The Merlin wedge has not been tested or defended against Coupa.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: No buyer expectations, decision criteria, or fit benchmarks documented. Product scope (eProcurement, eInvoicing, iSaaS, Merlin, Merlin Intake) is defined but buyer's prioritization, must-haves, and evaluation rubric are unknown. Cannot assess fit without discovery.

deltas.subject: SINGLE-THREADED RISK: Only 1 buyer-side contact role on this $250k, 9-product-line (including Merlin AI) deal. Michael Wang (VP Procurement, Decision Maker) is the sole named contact, and he has been silent for 9+ months. Account roster lists 20 Link contacts including COO (Kerry Hewson), CISO (Jerread Wright), CTO (Clark Ardern), CFO/FP&A (Rebecca Rebholz), VP Real Estate Ops (Amanda Boyd), Legal Senior Manager (Josette Williams), and multiple procurement managers and analysts—none mapped to this opp, none engaged on a call. If Michael ghosts or leaves, the deal dies. The immediate action is to demand introductions in the re-engagement call: ask Michael to bring his procurement team (Ian Mcintosh, Patrick Cullen, Riya Avaiya) to a discovery or Merlin demo, and to introduce the executive sponsor (likely COO or a C-level) who owns the business case and budget. Add every engaged contact as an OpportunityContactRole in Salesforce and build parallel relationships before advancing stage. (Source: SF OpportunityContactRole count=1; account contact list=20; zero multi-thread activity logged.)

deltas.to: MERLIN/AI POSITIONING GAP: Products in scope include Merlin and Merlin Intake (agentic AI for procurement automation), yet no discovery call, demo, or buyer con
deltas.subject: MERLIN/AI POSITIONING GAP: Products in scope include Merlin and Merlin Intake (agentic AI for procurement automation), yet no discovery call, demo, or buyer conversation has tested Link's AI appetite, use-case fit, or willingness to pay for AI capabilities. With Coupa as a named competitor—a legacy platform weak on autonomous AI—the Merlin wedge (Intake agent automating requisition routing and approvals, negotiation agent on supplier pricing, agentic sourcing vs manual workflows) is the single highest-leverage differentiator, but it has never been positioned with the buyer. The re-engagement call must lead with a Merlin Intake demo tailored to logistics/real-estate indirect procurement (Link's core business), quantify the ROI (cycle time reduction, procurement analyst time savings, spend visibility), and contrast it with Coupa's static, rules-based automation. If Link shows no AI appetite or understanding, either educate and build the business case or descope Merlin and reprice—do not carry an AI-tier deal without an AI buyer. (Source: SF Products__c includes Merlin + Merlin Intake; zero Avoma calls or SF notes on AI positioning; Competitors__c=Coupa, a Merlin wedge opportunity.)

value.how_we_win: Re-anchor the business case with Michael Wang: surface the procurement pain, quantify the cost of inaction (cycle time, manual workload, compliance risk, lost savings), tie it to a compelling event or executive mandate, and present Merlin Intake as the lever that delivers fast ROI and frees his team's capacity. If no compelling event exists or Michael remains unresponsive, disqualify and reallocate resources.

value.detail: Single-threaded to one VP Procurement (Michael Wang, Decision Maker) with no Economic Buyer, no champion, no multi-thread, and 9+ months of silence. If Michael has deprioritized the initiative, left the company, or is no longer sponsor, the deal is lost and we have no alternate entry point. Account roster lists 20 Link contacts including C-level (COO, CISO, CTO) and procurement team, but none are engaged.
value.source: SF OpportunityContactRole count=1; Avoma manifest shows zero engagement since Sep 2025; account contact list unused

value.detail: Close date 26 Mar 2027 (273 days out) with deal sitting in Qualified stage and zero forward progress in 2026. No discovery, demo, RFP, shortlist, or evaluation activity logged. Even with 9 months remaining, a cold deal in early Qualified with no buyer engagement, no MEDDPICC foundation, and no decision process has near-zero probability of closing on time—likely slips indefinitely or disqualifies.
value.source: SF StageName=Qualified, CloseDate=2027-03-26, CreatedDate=2026-02-17; 129 days in stage with no movement

value.detail: No Economic Buyer identified, no budget sign-off chain mapped, no executive sponsor confirmed. $250k SaaS+Merlin deal requires C-level or senior VP approval, but the authority and funding source are completely unknown. Michael Wang (VP Procurement) may control category spend but likely does not hold final budget authority for a six-figure multi-year software investment.
value.source: SF EB fields empty; no call or field evidence of budget authority or approval chain

value.detail: No commercials, pricing, or ROI discussion on record. Amount $250k is likely a placeholder (round number, no line-item detail). With zero discovery calls and no quantified pain or business case, the buyer has no anchor for pricing expectations and no financial justification to defend the spend internally. Coupa is a named competitor; without a transparent pricing conversation and a differentiated ROI (Merlin AI value), Zycus risks being priced out or undercut.
value.source: SF Amount=$250k (round number, no line items); no pricing, ROI, or commercial conversation logged

value.detail: Do-nothing / abandoned initiative risk. With 9 months of silence, no logged buyer activity, and no evidence the P2P project is live or funded, the highest probability outcome is that Link paused or cancelled the initiative and never informed Zycus. The deal may be a ghost opportunity carried in Pipeline because no disqualification conversation occurred.
value.source: Zero buyer engagement since Sep 2025; no calls, emails, or SF activity in 2026; Next Step 12 Jun is rep outreach, not buyer-initiated

value.flag: COLD DEAL RE-ENGAGEMENT: 9-month silence since Sep 2025 booth encounter (not recorded) with zero buyer calls, emails, or logged activity in 2026. Before investing further effort, run a direct re-qualification call with Michael Wang: reconfirm Link's P2P initiative is live, surface the current procurement pain and business case, map the shortlist and decision timeline, and assess his willingness to sponsor and multi-thread. If he is unresponsive or the initiative is paused/cancelled, disqualify immediately and reallocate resources to live deals. (Source: Avoma manifest shows 1 touchpoint 16 Sep 2025, zero since; SF LastActivityDate null; Next Step 12 Jun 2026 is rep outreach, not buyer engagement.)

value.flag: SINGLE-THREADED RISK: Only 1 buyer-side contact role on this $250k, 9-product-line (including Merlin AI) deal. Michael Wang (VP Procurement, Decision Maker) is the sole named contact, and he has been silent for 9+ months. Account roster lists 20 Link contacts including COO (Kerry Hewson), CISO (Jerread Wright), CTO (Clark Ardern), CFO/FP&A (Rebecca Rebholz), VP Real Estate Ops (Amanda Boyd), Legal Senior Manager (Josette Williams), and multiple procurement managers and analysts—none mapped to this opp, none engaged on a call. If Michael ghosts or leaves, the deal dies. The immediate action is to demand introductions in the re-engagement call: ask Michael to bring his procurement team (Ian Mcintosh, Patrick Cullen, Riya Avaiya) to a discovery or Merlin demo, and to introduce the executive sponsor (likely COO or a C-level) who owns the business case and budget. Add every engaged contact as an OpportunityContactRole in Salesforce and build parallel relationships before advancing stage. (Source: SF OpportunityContactRole count=1; account contact list=20; zero multi-thread activity logged.)

value.flag: MERLIN/AI POSITIONING GAP: Products in scope include Merlin and Merlin Intake (agentic AI for procurement automation), yet no discovery call, demo, or buyer conversation has tested Link's AI appetite, use-case fit, or willingness to pay for AI capabilities. With Coupa as a named competitor—a legacy platform weak on autonomous AI—the Merlin wedge (Intake agent automating requisition routing and approvals, negotiation agent on supplier pricing, agentic sourcing vs manual workflows) is the single highest-leverage differentiator, but it has never been positioned with the buyer. The re-engagement call must lead with a Merlin Intake demo tailored to logistics/real-estate indirect procurement (Link's core business), quantify the ROI (cycle time reduction, procurement analyst time savings, spend visibility), and contrast it with Coupa's static, rules-based automation. If Link shows no AI appetite or understanding, either educate and build the business case or descope Merlin and reprice—do not carry an AI-tier deal without an AI buyer. (Source: SF Products__c includes Merlin + Merlin Intake; zero Avoma calls or SF notes on AI positioning; Competitors__c=Coupa, a Merlin wedge opportunity.)
